title: Add option to prettify systemd units
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Systemd units generated by `podman generate systemd --new --name --files POD` are useful but not human friendly, as all options are printed in one line. It would be great to have a `--pretty` flag (or similar) so different options are printed on different lines (with some indentation if possible) to improve readability.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman generate systemd --new --name --files POD` 

2. Run `systemctl edit --full pod-POD.service`

**Describe the results you received:**
```ini
# container-authentik-server.service
# autogenerated by Podman 3.4.4
# Sun Feb 20 22:03:19 CET 2022

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-authentik-server.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers
BindsTo=pod-authentik.service container-authentik-postgres.service container-authentik-redis.service
After=pod-authentik.service container-authentik-postgres.service container-authentik-redis.service

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id --cgroups=no-conmon --rm --pod-id-file %t/pod-authentik.pod-id --sdnotify=conmon -d --replace --name authentik-server --requires authentik-postgres --requires authentik-redis --memory=1G --entrypoint "[\"/lifecycle/ak\", \"server\"]" --env-file /home/podman/containers/config/authentik/server/env --volume /home/podman/containers/storage/authentik/server/media:/media --volume /home/podman/containers/storage/authentik/server/templates:/templates ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:latest
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -f --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```ini
# container-authentik-server.service
# autogenerated by Podman 3.4.4
# Sun Feb 20 22:03:19 CET 2022

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-authentik-server.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers
BindsTo=pod-authentik.service \
    container-authentik-postgres.service \
    container-authentik-redis.service
After=pod-authentik.service \
    container-authentik-postgres.service \
    container-authentik-redis.service

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=/bin/rm -f %t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run \
    --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id \
    --cgroups=no-conmon \
    --rm \
    --pod-id-file %t/pod-authentik.pod-id \
    --sdnotify=conmon \
    -d \
    --replace \
    --name authentik-server \
    --requires authentik-postgres \
    --requires authentik-redis \
    --memory=1G \
    --entrypoint "[\"/lifecycle/ak\", \"server\"]" \
    --env-file /home/podman/containers/config/authentik/server/env \
    --volume /home/podman/containers/storage/authentik/server/media:/media \
    --volume /home/podman/containers/storage/authentik/server/templates:/templates \
    ghcr.io/goauthentik/server:latest
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -f --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.4
Git Commit:   f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933
Built:        Thu Dec  9 20:21:17 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
not relevant
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
not relevant
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

not relevant

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @AlphaJack!

I really like the idea. It'll need some investigation on how to prettify. Probably via the templates, @Luap99 ?
Yes like that idea, I don't think we need a option for this. We should do it by default I do not see any harm or why someone would want to stick with the old format.

> Yes like that idea, I don't think we need a option for this. We should do it by default I do not see any harm or why someone would want to stick with the old format.

I concur. If we can make it pretty, let's make it pretty :) There's no guarantee on the output format other than systemd must be able to eat it.
For the implementation I think we can replace `[space]-` with `[space][backslash][newline][tab]-`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan @vrothberg I am interested in working on this.

Is it possible to also prettify pod units other than container ones?
Also, is there no option to have the image on a new line instead of the same line of the last option?
It would be best to open new issues on this, or better yet a PR.
